en: After the uproar ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, and after encouraging them, he said farewell and departed for Macedonia.
zh: 騷亂平息以後，保羅派人把門徒請了來，勸勉一番，就辭別起行，往馬其頓去。

en: When he had gone through those regions and had given them much encouragement, he came to Greece.
zh: 他走遍那一帶地方，用許多話勸勉眾人，然後來到希臘。

en: There he spent three months, and when a plot was made against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
zh: 他在那裡住了三個月，正要坐船往敘利亞去的時候，有些猶太人設計要害他，他就決意路經馬其頓回去。

en: Sopater of Berea, the son of Pyrrhus from Berea, accompanied him; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy; and the Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
zh: 與他同行的有比里亞人畢羅斯的兒子所巴特，帖撒羅尼迦人亞里達古和西公都，特庇人該猶和提摩太，亞西亞人推基古和特羅非摩。

en: These went on ahead and were waiting for us at Troas,
zh: 這些人先走，在特羅亞等候我們。

en: but we sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and in five days we came to them at Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
zh: 至於我們，過了除酵節才從腓立比開船，五天後到特羅亞他們那裡，逗留了七天。

en: On the first day of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day, and he prolonged his speech until midnight.
zh: 禮拜日，我們聚會擘餅的時候，保羅對大家講道，他因第二天就要起行，就一直講到半夜。

en: There were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered.
zh: 我們聚會的那座樓上，有許多燈火。

en: And a young man named Eutychus, sitting at the window, sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked still longer. And being overcome by sleep, he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead.
zh: 有一個少年人，名叫猶推古，坐在窗臺上，因為保羅講得很長，他很困倦，沉沉地睡著了，就從三樓跌下來；把他扶起來的時候，已經死了。

en: But Paul went down and bent over him, and taking him in his arms, said, "Do not be alarmed, for his life is in him."
zh: 保羅走下去，伏在他身上；把他抱住，說：“你們不要驚慌，他還活著。”

en: And when Paul had gone up and had broken bread and eaten, he conversed with them a long while, until daybreak, and so departed.
zh: 隨即回到樓上，擘餅吃了，又講了很久，直到天亮才走。

en: And they took the youth away alive, and were not a little comforted.
zh: 他們把活著的孩子帶走，得到很大的安慰。

en: But going ahead to the ship, we set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there, for so he had arranged, intending himself to go by land.
zh: 我們先上船，開往亞朔，照著保羅的安排，要在那邊接他，因為他自己要走陸路去。

en: And when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and went to Mitylene.
zh: 他在亞朔與我們會合，我們接他上船，去到米推利尼。

en: And sailing from there we came the following day opposite Chios; the next day we touched at Samos; and the day after that we went to Miletus.
zh: 從那裡開船，第二天到了基阿對面，過了一天就在撒摩靠岸，再過一天到了米利都。

en: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus, so that he might not have to spend time in Asia, for he was hastening to be at Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
zh: 原來保羅決定越過以弗所，免得在亞西亞耽擱時間，因為他希望能在五旬節趕到耶路撒冷。

en: Now from Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called the elders of the church to come to him.
zh: 他從米利都派人去以弗所，請教會的長老來。

en: And when they came to him, he said to them: "You yourselves know how I lived among you the whole time from the first day that I set foot in Asia,
zh: 他們到了，保羅說：“你們知道，自從我到了亞西亞的第一天，我一直怎樣跟你們相處，

en: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials that happened to me through the plots of the Jews;
zh: 怎樣服事主，凡事謙卑，常常流淚，忍受猶太人謀害的試煉。

en: how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, and teaching you in public and from house to house,
zh: 你們也知道，我從來沒有留下一件有益的事，不在眾人面前或在各人家裡告訴你們，教導你們。

en: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks of repentance toward God and of faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
zh: 我對猶太人和希臘人都作過見證，要他們悔改歸向 神，信靠我們的主耶穌。

en: And now, behold, I am going to Jerusalem, constrained by the Spirit, not knowing what will happen to me there,
zh: 現在，我的心靈受到催逼，要去耶路撒冷，我不知道在那裡會遭遇甚麼事，

en: except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that imprisonment and afflictions await me.
zh: 只知道在各城裡聖靈都向我指明，說有捆鎖和患難在等著我。

en: But I do not account my life of any value nor as precious to myself, if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the gospel of the grace of God.
zh: 但我並不珍惜自己的性命，只求跑完我的路程，完成我從主耶穌所領受的職分，為 神恩惠的福音作見證。

en: And now, behold, I know that none of you among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom will see my face again.
zh: “我曾在你們那裡走遍各地，宣揚 神的國。現在，我知道你們眾人不會再見我的面了。

en: Therefore I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all of you,
zh: 所以我今天向你們作證，我是清白的，與眾人的罪 無關。

en: for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
zh: 因為 神的全部計劃，我已經毫無保留地傳給你們了。

en: Pay careful attention to yourselves and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to care for the church of God, which he obtained with his own blood.
zh: 聖靈既然立你們為全群的監督，牧養 神用自己的血所贖來的教會，你們就應當為自己謹慎，也為全群謹慎。

en: I know that after my departure fierce wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock;
zh: 我知道在我離開之後，必有兇暴的豺狼進入你們中間，不顧惜羊群。

en: and from among your own selves will arise men speaking twisted things, to draw away the disciples after them.
zh: 你們自己中間也必有人起來，講些歪曲悖謬的話，引誘門徒跟從他們。

en: Therefore be alert, remembering that for three years I did not cease night or day to admonish everyone with tears.
zh: 所以你們應當警醒，記念我三年之久，晝夜不停地帶著眼淚勸戒你們各人。

en: And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
zh: 現在我把你們交託給 神和他恩惠的道；這道能建立你們，也能在所有成聖的人中賜基業給你們。

en: I coveted no one's silver or gold or apparel.
zh: 我從來沒有貪圖任何人的金銀或衣服。

en: You yourselves know that these hands ministered to my necessities and to those who were with me.
zh: 我這兩隻手，供應了我和同伴的需要，這是你們自己知道的。

en: In all things I have shown you that by working hard in this way we must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he himself said, 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.'"
zh: 我凡事以身作則，你們必須照樣辛勞，扶助軟弱的人，並且記念主耶穌的話：‘施比受更為有福。’”

en: And when he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
zh: 他說了這些話，就跪下來同大家一起禱告。

en: And there was much weeping on the part of all; they embraced Paul and kissed him,
zh: 眾人都痛哭，抱著保羅，與他親吻。

en: being sorrowful most of all because of the word he had spoken, that they would not see his face again. And they accompanied him to the ship.
zh: 他們最傷心的，是保羅說他們不會再見他的面那句話。最後他們送他上了船。
